title: salix × matsumurae
intro: Salix × matsumurae, a hybrid willow tree, is a cross between the Japanese weeping willow (Salix japonica) and the white willow (Salix alba). Its bark contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, which has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties. The medicinal properties of Salix × matsumurae have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and headaches, providing relief from inflammation and pain. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
